project_type: Hot-swap controller (Analog Devices ADM1281-3) with three parallel PSMN0R925YLC power FETs
problem_hypothesis: product works ok from user perspective but fails conducted emission test
notes: ADM1281-3 hot-swap controller. Three parallel power MOSFETs (M1/M2/M3 = PSMN0R925YLC). Sense resistors R2 + R3 = 0.5 mOhm each. PCB is 2-layer (FR-4 1.6mm typical). 1 m input cable. LISN-based pre-compliance setup. NOT a DC/DC switching converter — there is no PWM; conducted EMI risk comes from the FET turn-on slew rate. The testbench feeds a DC 12 V rail through the LISN + cable, so the controller's power-up inrush occurs near t=0 (V(MEAS) activity peaks around 0.08 ms); the run uses a fine 5 ns timestep over a 1 ms window (`.tran 0 1m 0 5n`, ~100 MHz Nyquist) so the 150 kHz – 30 MHz CISPR band B resolves for the conducted-EMI detector analysis.
